notes: Known-account skill discovery reads hash links from /u/{account} profile pages, then follows those detail URLs.
The exported dataset is pruned to owners that publish at least one AISA-using skill or plugin.
Within AISA-owner accounts, non-AISA items are retained in the dataset for context.
AISA detection is inferred from README and rendered page content.
Plugin discovery uses public catalog pages, manual known-plugin seeds, and heuristic plugin slug guesses derived from AISA skills.
Global discovery currently scans the first 3 pages of ClawHub skills and plugins.
Suspicious status is inferred from rendered security scan output.
Original skill download links are extracted from "Download zip" buttons when the detail page exposes them.

items.name: Web Search by Tavily

optimizationAdvice: Use clawhub-skill-optimizer: add a frontmatter description with explicit "Use when:" trigger wording.
Use clawhub-skill-optimizer: add a "When to use" section with 3-5 concrete invocation scenarios.
Align metadata, homepage, source repo, and published version so ClawHub scanner sees consistent provenance.
Keep the package AISA-only: declare only AISA env vars, document the exact API scope, and remove legacy credential paths.

items.suspiciousReason: The package is a coherent Twitter/X relay client that contacts api.aisa.one and requires an AISA_API_KEY, but the registry metadata and package frontmatter disagree about required env and versioning—confirm the missing environment declaration and trust in the relay before installing.
optimizationAdvice: Align metadata, homepage, source repo, and published version so ClawHub scanner sees consistent provenance.
Keep the package AISA-only: declare only AISA env vars, document the exact API scope, and remove legacy credential paths.
Minimize the release bundle to runtime files, SKILL.md, and essential metadata; remove packaging/debug helpers.
items.readmeSnippet: --- name: openclaw-aisa-twitter description: Search X/Twitter profiles, tweets, trends, and approved engagement actions through the AISA relay. Use when: the user asks for Twitter/X research, posting, likes, follows, or related workflows without sharing passwords. Supports read APIs, OAuth-gated posting, and follow or

items.name: openclaw-aisa-youtube-aisa
items.description: Search YouTube videos, channels, and trends via AISA API using Python or curl with locale and filter options, requiring AISA_API_KEY.
items.suspiciousReason: The skill's code and SKILL.md match the described YouTube search purpose and only require an AISA API key, but the registry metadata incorrectly omits that required credential and overall provenance (homepage/owner) is thin — this mismatch warrants caution before installing.
optimizationAdvice: Align metadata, homepage, source repo, and published version so ClawHub scanner sees consistent provenance.
Keep the package AISA-only: declare only AISA env vars, document the exact API scope, and remove legacy credential paths.
Minimize the release bundle to runtime files, SKILL.md, and essential metadata; remove packaging/debug helpers.
items.readmeSnippet: --- name: openclaw-aisa-youtube-aisa description: Search YouTube videos, channels, and trends through the AISA YouTube SERP client. items.name: Prediction Market Arbitrage

items.suspiciousReason: The skill's code and SKILL.md consistently use the AIsa API and require an AISA_API_KEY (which is appropriate for its stated purpose), but the registry-level metadata omitted that requirement — a packaging inconsistency you should verify before installing.
